title: KCET Admit Card 2026 Out

detail.title: KCET Admit Card 2026 Out
detail.short_info: KCET Admit Card 2026 Out for Karnataka Common Entrance Test on the official website - kea.kar.nic.in from April 10, 2026. Download your KCET hall ticket, check exam date, exam centre and important instructions for the Karnataka CET 2026 exam.

content.text: Karnataka Examinations Authority has issued the KCET Admit Card 2026 for all eligible candidates registered for the Karnataka Common Entrance Test. The hall ticket is now available in online mode only, and no physical admit card will be sent by post. KCET Admit Card 2026 has been released online on the official website - kea.kar.nic.in - and through the dedicated login link shared by KEA.

Candidates must download and print their KCET hall ticket well before the exam date, as it is mandatory for entry into the examination centre. After downloading the admit card, students should cross-check their personal details, exam centre address and exam schedule, and immediately contact KEA in case of any discrepancy.

KCET Admit Card 2026 is one of the most important documents for candidates appearing in Karnataka CET, as it confirms their test centre, shift timing and roll number. The highlights below offer a quick snapshot for users and search engines.

rows: KCET Admit Card 2026 / KCET 2026 Hall Ticket
Candidate details, KCET roll number, exam date and time, exam centre, instructions
KCET 2026 admit card printout, valid photo ID proof, other documents as instructed by KEA

sections.heading: KCET Admit Card 2026 Important Dates & Exam Schedule

items: Admit Card Release: April 10, 2026.
Kannada Language Test: April 22, 2026.
KCET Exam (Physics & Chemistry): April 23, 2026.
KCET Exam (Mathematics / Biology): April 24, 2026.

content.text: The Karnataka Examinations Authority has provided a dedicated login page for candidates to download the KCET hall ticket. This navigational section helps candidates reach the correct official pages without confusion.

sections.heading: How to Download KCET Admit Card 2026

content.text: A clear and simple step-by-step guide helps candidates complete the download process without errors and also aligns with common search queries like “how to download KCET hall ticket”.
Step-by-step Process to Download KCET 2026 Hall Ticket

items: Open a web browser on your mobile or computer and visit the KCET 2026 admit card login page at https://cetonline.karnataka.gov.in/UGCET_Admit_Card_2026/Forms/Login.aspx.
On the login screen, enter your KCET 2026 application number in the specified field.
Enter your date of birth or password, exactly as used during the KCET registration process.
Double-check the details and click on the "Login" or "Submit" button.
After successful login, look for the option "Download KCET Admit Card 2026" or "KCET Hall Ticket".
Click on the download button; your KCET admit card will open in PDF format on the screen.
Save the PDF on your device and take at least one clear A4-size printout, preferably in colour, to carry to the exam centre.

content.text: If you forget your application number, you should first check your registered email, SMS or printout of the KCET application form. In case of technical errors on the site, wait a while and try again or use another browser and device.

sections.heading: Details Mentioned on KCET Admit Card 2026

content.text: Candidates should thoroughly verify all the details printed on the KCET hall ticket to avoid issues on the exam day.
Key details printed on KCET Admit Card 2026 typically include:

items: Candidate name and photograph
KCET 2026 application number and roll number
Date of birth, category and gender
Exam date and reporting time
Exam centre name, code and full address
Subjects or papers to be attempted (for example, Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics/Biology as applicable)
Space for invigilator signature and candidate signature
Important exam day rules and instructions

content.text: If any major detail on the admit card does not match the details provided in your application form, you should contact KEA through the official helpdesk channels as early as possible.

sections.heading: KCET 2026 Exam Day Guidelines (As per Admit Card)

content.text: This section answers frequent user doubts related to what to carry, when to report and exam hall rules, which often appear in “People also ask” on Google.
Some important exam day guidelines for KCET 2026 are:

items: Reach the exam centre well before the reporting time mentioned on the admit card to complete frisking and verification procedures.
Carry a printed copy of KCET Admit Card 2026 along with a valid photo ID proof such as Aadhaar card, PAN card, passport, voter ID, driving licence or school/college ID (as permitted by KEA).
Do not carry electronic items like mobile phones, smart watches, calculators, earphones or any other gadgets inside the examination hall.
Stationery instructions (such as whether you need to bring a black/blue ball pen or any other item) should be followed exactly as mentioned on the admit card and in the KEA instructions.
Follow the instructions given by the invigilators and avoid any kind of malpractice or unfair means, as that may lead to cancellation of your candidature.

content.text: Reading and understanding all instructions printed on the admit card before the exam day will help avoid last-minute confusion and stress.

sections.heading: KCET 2026 Admit Card - Eligibility and Issuance Note

content.text: KCET Admit Card 2026 is issued only to those candidates who:

items: Successfully completed the KCET 2026 application form,
Paid the required application fee within the deadline, and
Fulfilled the basic eligibility conditions prescribed by KEA for appearing in the exam.

content.text: The availability of the admit card does not guarantee admission; it only permits the candidate to appear for the entrance examination. Final admission will be based on KCET scores, counselling, document verification and other criteria laid down by the Karnataka Examinations Authority and participating institutions.

sections.heading: Important Instructions About KCET Admit Card 2026

content.text: To help candidates avoid common mistakes, you can highlight the most crucial points in one place.

items: KCET 2026 admit card will not be sent via post or courier; it must be downloaded online.
Keep multiple copies of the printed admit card in case one is lost or damaged.
Ensure that the photo and signature on the admit card are clearly visible; if not, re-download and reprint the document.
Do not write or make unnecessary markings on the admit card, except where signatures are required.
Preserve the KCET admit card until the completion of counselling and admission, as it may be needed during verification.
